title: Understanding Gravity: A Basic Guide
summary: Gravity is a natural force that attracts two bodies with mass towards each other. It explains why objects fall to the ground and keeps planets in orbit.

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: Gravity is a fundamental force of nature that pulls all matter with mass or energy together. It's the reason why we stay grounded on Earth and not float off into space.
key_points: It acts between any two objects in the universe
The strength depends on their masses and distances

sections.heading: How it works
sections.body: Gravity is a result of massive objects bending spacetime with their mass. This curvature directs other smaller objects towards the larger one.
key_points: Objects follow these curves, resulting in attraction

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.body: Without gravity, life as we know would not exist. It keeps our atmosphere intact and allows for the formation of structures like galaxies.
key_points: It's vital to planetary systems stability

key_facts: Gravity is universal; it acts everywhere in the universe
